change_summary: Backlog prioritization: two dogfood triggers fired the same day (2026-07-23) and they are the two edges of one end-of-phase-1 verdict gate.
scope: docs/backlog.md against the phase files it targets (phase-1-assess.md, phase-2-baseline.md, commands/migrate.md); the decision of what to implement now.

reviewers.name: Elon Musk
reviewers.verdict: You already know what to build — two triggers fired the same day and they're the same change; stop writing it down and wire the phase-1 verdict.
reviewers.hard_truth: Your own dogfooding found the pipeline does something dumb on the two most likely non-happy-path inputs — so why is that a backlog note and not the current diff?

assessment.notes: Every item carries an explicit profitability trigger and YAGNI otherwise; non-adoptions recorded. Rare and good.
Two of seven triggers fired via dogfooding; five have not. The file answers its own question.
The two fired items are one classifier at the end of phase 1, not two tickets — risk of splitting into contradictory logic.
/migrate deploys Blazor for a CLI on an already-modern target, and phase 2 refuses TFM-lag-red repos it was built for.
Healthy per-wave lessons, but the last two commits are backlog notes from dogfooding, not the fix they describe.
The two dogfood cases prove the bug today but nothing pins them as regression cases for the new gate.

findings.title: /migrate produces nonsense on an already-modern target (no ALREADY_MODERN verdict)
findings.description: Phase 1 step 6 always recommends 'latest LTS TFM', even net10.0 -> net10.0, and nothing stops /migrate from running an empty phase 3 (retarget), an empty phase 5 (idioms already modern), then a phase 7 that deploys Blazor to GitHub Pages for a tool with no web surface. audit-executive.md defines only 'obsolete TFM' and 'UI rewrite' profiles — there is no 'healthy, nothing to migrate' profile. Dogfood-proven on Atypical-Consulting/StaticWGen (net10.0 everywhere, SDK pinned, packages current).
findings.recommendation: Add a modernity gate at the end of phase 1: if all TFMs are in {latest LTS, requested target} AND no out-of-support runtime AND no obsolete-API cluster (SYSLIB/packages.config/BinaryFormatter), set recommended target = 'none, already up to date', write verdict: ALREADY_MODERN into assessment.md, and have /migrate stop after phase 1 (like /migrate-assess). Route a modern-but-wants-a-report case explicitly to /migrate-verify (phase 6) — modern != clean; StaticWGen's only successful restore surfaced NU1903 (System.Security.Cryptography.Xml 9.0.0, high-severity transitive).

findings.title: Phase 2 green-baseline gate blocks the exact repos the tool exists for (TFM lagging its packages)
findings.description: Phase 2 step 1 demands a green build/restore 'before touching' and says a red baseline is 'a prerequisite task, not part of the migration'. But when a bot (Renovate) pushes the package graph past the TFM — net9.0 + EF Core 10 / ASP.NET 10 (net10-only packages) — restore is impossible (NU1202) BEFORE any migration. The gate cannot hold: the net9->net10 retarget IS the prerequisite for a green restore, not the reverse. Dogfood-proven on phmatray/DotnetChain (PR #64 merged into dev, 88 tests green).
findings.recommendation: Have phase 1/2 recognize the 'baseline red caused by a TFM lagging its packages' case (signature: NU1202 'package X supports netN.0' on a TFM netM.0 with M<N), set verdict: RED_BY_TFM_LAG, and establish the first green POST-retarget as the recorded baseline. Corollary (rules 5 + 9): making a long-broken build compile can expose large pre-existing, framework-unrelated breakage (DotnetChain: half-finished domain refactor, 536 CS errors) — the kit repairs everything verifiable and names the edges without inventing missing behavior.

findings.title: Findings 1 and 2 are one end-of-phase-1 classifier, not two tickets
findings.description: The backlog itself notes item #7 is the sister of item #6 — same step, inverse symptom. Both are decisions made at the single point where phase 1 hands off to phase 2. Shipping them as two independent PRs will grow two overlapping pieces of classification logic that can disagree (e.g. a repo that is both current-TFM and red-on-restore). The plumbing to act on the verdict already exists: /migrate-assess = phase 1 only, /migrate-verify = phase 6 only.
findings.recommendation: Wire ONE verdict field on assessment.md — verdict: {ALREADY_MODERN | RED_BY_TFM_LAG | NORMAL} — computed at the end of phase 1, and branch /migrate on it at exactly one place: ALREADY_MODERN -> stop (offer /migrate-verify), RED_BY_TFM_LAG -> retarget-then-baseline, NORMAL -> proceed to phase 2 unchanged. Update the SKILL.md pipeline table and Artifact contract to document the verdict. No new scope command.

findings.title: New verdict gate has no regression lock (dogfood cases will be forgotten)
findings.description: StaticWGen (ALREADY_MODERN) and DotnetChain (RED_BY_TFM_LAG) prove the two verdicts today, but nothing pins them as regression cases. In three months the classifier can silently drift and re-introduce the empty-phase deploy or the false-negative baseline block, with no test to catch it.
findings.recommendation: Capture each dogfood case as a documented assessment fixture: the input signature (TFMs, package graph, diagnostics) and the expected verdict. Add them to the phase-1 reference (or a golden test alongside the other kit golden tests) so the verdict logic is covered the way followups (16/16) and the report generator already are.

findings.title: The other five backlog items have unfired triggers — keep them unbuilt
findings.description: sync-artifacts (needs ~5 migrated repos), mcp-list timeout (needs a first freeze), the 6-skill trigger bench (needs the next description edit), FR->EN reference translation (needs a non-francophone user), and followups trigger tuning (needs a first real-session under-trigger) all have triggers that have NOT fired. Building any now is speculative work against the backlog's own YAGNI rule.
findings.recommendation: Do nothing on these five this cycle. Leave them in the file with their triggers intact. Revisit each only when its named trigger actually fires.

actions.title: Ship ONE end-of-phase-1 verdict gate covering both symptoms (ALREADY_MODERN stop + RED_BY_TFM_LAG retarget-then-baseline), NORMAL unchanged
Pin StaticWGen and DotnetChain as documented regression cases for the verdict so it can't drift
Leave the other five backlog items unbuilt until their triggers fire

praise: The backlog is disciplined the way most teams never manage: every item names the trigger that makes it profitable, and the non-adoptions (Idea Tree, interaction modes, novelty search) are recorded so a closed decision survives future sessions.
You found both of these bugs by dogfooding your own tool on real repos (StaticWGen, DotnetChain) — the fastest possible signal, and the reason you can prioritize from evidence instead of taste.
